# o2-scalpel-csharp

Scalpel refactor MCP server for C# via csharp-ls

## Install

```bash
claude plugin install o2-scalpel-csharp --from o2-scalpel
```

## Requirements

- Claude Code >= 1.0.0
- LSP server: `csharp-ls` on `$PATH`
- File extensions handled: .cs, .csx

## Facades

| Facade | Summary |
|---|---|
| `scalpel_extract` | Extract selection into a method or variable |
| `scalpel_inline` | Inline a method at all call sites |
| `scalpel_rename` | Rename a symbol across the workspace |
| `scalpel_organize_imports` | Remove unused using directives and sort import order |
| `scalpel_rewrite` | Apply rewrite transformations (convert, invert, etc.) |
| `scalpel_fix_lints` | Apply all auto-fixable diagnostics (quickfix) |

## Slash commands

This plugin ships two slash commands under `commands/`:

| Command | Scope | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `/o2-scalpel-csharp-dashboard` | this plugin | Discovers the running `scalpel-csharp` MCP server's dashboard port (`pgrep` + `lsof`) and opens it in the browser. **Lazy-bind caveat**: the engine binds the dashboard only after the first `scalpel_*` tool call against this server — if discovery reports "not yet bound", invoke any facade (e.g. `scalpel_workspace_health`) and re-run. |
| `/o2-scalpel-update` | engine-wide | Force-refreshes the uvx-cached `o2-scalpel-engine` to the latest commit on `main` (`uvx --refresh ...`), updates `~/.cache/o2-scalpel/installed-sha`, clears the status-line update indicator, and prints restart guidance for any running `scalpel-*` MCP servers. Does **not** auto-restart MCP servers. The body is byte-identical across all 23 plugin trees so Claude Code's plugin registry surfaces a single command. |

## Hooks

This plugin ships SessionStart hooks under `hooks/` (wired through `hooks/hooks.json`):

| Hook | Trigger | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `verify-scalpel-csharp.sh` | SessionStart | Verifies that `csharp-ls` is on `$PATH`. Exits non-zero when missing so Claude Code surfaces an actionable error. |
| `check-scalpel-update.sh` | SessionStart | Probes `git ls-remote https://github.com/o2alexanderfedin/o2-scalpel-engine.git HEAD` (no auth, public repo) and updates the shared cache `~/.cache/o2-scalpel/update-check.json`. Throttled to **1 network call per 6 hours** across all enabled scalpel-* plugins. Seeds `installed-sha` from upstream on first run so users don't see a false "update available" prompt. |

The plugin also ships a standalone POSIX-shell script `hooks/scalpel-statusline.sh` (no Python, no uvx, < 5 ms) you can wire into `~/.claude/settings.json` as `statusLine.command` — it reads the cache JSON and emits the yellow `⬆ /o2-scalpel-update` segment when an update is available, nothing otherwise.

For the full status-line setup recipe (one-time `settings.json` snippet, cache-file reference, disable instructions) see [STATUSLINE.md](https://github.com/o2alexanderfedin/o2-scalpel/blob/main/docs/reviews/2026-05-01-scalpel-vs-serena-routing-audit/STATUSLINE.md).

## Skills

This plugin ships skills under `skills/` so Claude knows when to call each facade.
